title: Xeraea globosa
taxonomy: Xeraea globosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Xeraea and is native to South America. The classification of Xeraea globosa is further detailed as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Gentianales, family - Apocynaceae, genus - Xeraea, species - X. globosa. intro: Xeraea globosa, also known as the 'Golden Snail Mel' or 'Mimosa Pudica', is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Xeraea globosa include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
